perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Dispute Resolution. Decisions regarding Study design, changes and/or additions to the initially-agreed Protocols must be unanimous, with each Party exercising one vote each, and in the absence of such unanimity the status quo shall be maintained. For all other matters before the JSC, a unanimous decision, with each Party exercising one vote, is required; provided, that, if unanimity cannot be achieved regarding such other matters, then LBIO's chief executive officer may make the decision on behalf of the JSC, provided that LBIO's chief executive officer will first make a good faith effort to consult with a designated executive at MD Anderson to resolve such matter.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Dispute Resolution. Decisions regarding Study design, changes and/or additions to the initially-agreed Protocols must be unanimous, with each Party exercising one vote each, and in the absence of such unanimity the status quo shall be maintained. For all other matters before the JSC, a unanimous decision, with each Party exercising one vote, is required; provided, that, if unanimity cannot be achieved regarding such other matters, then the decision will be determined by a coin toss overseen by a mutually agreed-upon third party, provided that both parties first make a good faith effort to consult with each other to resolve such matter.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that in the event of a disagreement, LBIO's CEO can make the final decision. The modified text contradicts this by stating that a coin toss will decide the issue. This creates uncertainty because it is unclear how disputes will be resolved, since in the Section 1.4, it is being presented that the final decision for those other matters is up to a coin toss, where the previous it was up to LBIO's CEO.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Dispute Resolution. Decisions regarding Study design, changes and/or additions to the initially-agreed Protocols must be unanimous, with each Party exercising one vote each, and in the absence of such unanimity the status quo shall be maintained. For all other matters before the JSC, a unanimous decision, with each Party exercising one vote, is required; provided, that, if unanimity cannot be achieved regarding such other matters, then the decision will be determined by a coin toss overseen by a mutually agreed-upon third party, provided that both parties first make a good faith effort to consult with each other to resolve such matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction in the dispute resolution process. The original text designates LBIO's CEO as the final decision-maker in case of a deadlock, while the modified text states that a coin toss will determine the outcome. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict, making it unclear which method of dispute resolution should be followed. Both texts are in the same Section 1.4, but the modified text changes the procedure to be used for resolving a deadlock. This contradicts the original intent and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Confidential Information. (a) "Confidential Information" means any proprietary or confidential information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including research, product plans, products, services, customer lists and customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing, distribution and sales methods and systems, sales and profit figures, finances and other business information disclosed by a Party or its Representatives ("Disclosing Party") to the other Party or its Representatives ("Receiving Party"), whether in writing, orally or by drawings or inspection of documents or other tangible property; provided that: (i) Confidential Information shall not include any of the foregoing items to the extent that (1) they are or have become publicly known and made generally available through no wrongful act of Receiving Party, (2) they were known to Receiving Party prior to disclosure by Disclosing Party, as evidenced by pre-existing written records promptly provided to Disclosing Party by Receiving Party, (3) they were disclosed to Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality by a third party having a lawful right to make such disclosure, or (4) they were developed by Receiving Party without use or aid of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, and (ii) the results of the Research (including the contents of each Report and any Inventions) shall be deemed to be LBIO's Confidential Information, subject to MD Anderson's right to publish any Research data and information as set forth in and in accordance with Section 6.4, MD Anderson's right to use any Inventions (and any Work) as set forth in and in accordance with Section 7.2, and MD Anderson's right to use any Research data and information for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure and for any purpose thereafter. LBIO shall be deemed the Disclosing Party with respect to such results of the Research, regardless of the Party initially disclosing the same.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Confidential Information. (a) "Confidential Information" means any proprietary or confidential information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including research, product plans, products, services, customer lists and customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing, distribution and sales methods and systems, sales and profit figures, finances and other business information disclosed by a Party or its Representatives ("Disclosing Party") to the other Party or its Representatives ("Receiving Party"), whether in writing, orally or by drawings or inspection of documents or other tangible property; provided that: (i) Confidential Information shall not include any of the foregoing items to the extent that (1) they are or have become publicly known and made generally available through no wrongful act of Receiving Party, (2) they were known to Receiving Party prior to disclosure by Disclosing Party, as evidenced by pre-existing written records promptly provided to Disclosing Party by Receiving Party, (3) they were disclosed to Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality by a third party having a lawful right to make such disclosure, or (4) they were developed by Receiving Party without use or aid of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, and (ii) the results of the Research (including the contents of each Report and any Inventions) shall be deemed to be LBIO's Confidential Information, subject to MD Anderson's right to publish any Research data and information as set forth in and in accordance with Section 6.4, MD Anderson's right to use any Inventions (and any Work) as set forth in and in accordance with Section 7.2, and MD Anderson's right to use any Research data and information for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure and for any purpose thereafter, unless LBIO provides written consent. LBIO shall be deemed the Disclosing Party with respect to such results of the Research, regardless of the Party initially disclosing the same.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds "unless LBIO provides written consent" at the end of the sentence, after MD Anderson has rights for Research data, contradicting the rights already given to MD Anderson. This introduces an element of uncertainty as to whether MD Anderson can truly utilize its rights without LBIO's consent, creating a conflict with the original agreement. The contradiction resides in the fact that originally, MD Anderson has free rights for Research Data, and with the added sentencem these are now being restricted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Confidential Information. (a) "Confidential Information" means any proprietary or confidential information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including research, product plans, products, services, customer lists and customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing, distribution and sales methods and systems, sales and profit figures, finances and other business information disclosed by a Party or its Representatives ("Disclosing Party") to the other Party or its Representatives ("Receiving Party"), whether in writing, orally or by drawings or inspection of documents or other tangible property; provided that: (i) Confidential Information shall not include any of the foregoing items to the extent that (1) they are or have become publicly known and made generally available through no wrongful act of Receiving Party, (2) they were known to Receiving Party prior to disclosure by Disclosing Party, as evidenced by pre-existing written records promptly provided to Disclosing Party by Receiving Party, (3) they were disclosed to Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality by a third party having a lawful right to make such disclosure, or (4) they were developed by Receiving Party without use or aid of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, and (ii) the results of the Research (including the contents of each Report and any Inventions) shall be deemed to be LBIO's Confidential Information, subject to MD Anderson's right to publish any Research data and information as set forth in and in accordance with Section 6.4, MD Anderson's right to use any Inventions (and any Work) as set forth in and in accordance with Section 7.2, and MD Anderson's right to use any Research data and information for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure and for any purpose thereafter, unless LBIO provides written consent. LBIO shall be deemed the Disclosing Party with respect to such results of the Research, regardless of the Party initially disclosing the same.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'unless LBIO provides written consent' directly contradicts MD Anderson's previously established rights to use and publish research data. The original text grants MD Anderson certain rights regarding research data, but the changed text introduces a condition (LBIO's consent) that significantly restricts those rights, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as the added language directly undermines the rights granted earlier in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Assignment of Inventions; Further Assurances. (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes]. MD Anderson further acknowledges and agrees that all original works of authorship that are made by MD Anderson (solely or jointly with others) in the performance of the Research, excluding any publication made in accordance with Section 6.4 (a "Work") and that are protectable by copyright are "works made for hire," as that term is defined in the United States Copyright Act. However, to the extent that any Work may not, by operation of any Laws, be a work made for hire, MD Anderson hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to such Work, including all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto, subject to MD Anderson's right to use such Work for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Assignment of Inventions; Further Assurances. (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes only with prior written approval from LBIO]. MD Anderson further acknowledges and agrees that all original works of authorship that are made by MD Anderson (solely or jointly with others) in the performance of the Research, excluding any publication made in accordance with Section 6.4 (a "Work") and that are protectable by copyright are "works made for hire," as that term is defined in the United States Copyright Act. However, to the extent that any Work may not, by operation of any Laws, be a work made for hire, MD Anderson hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to such Work, including all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto, subject to MD Anderson's right to use such Work for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure only with prior written approval from LBIO.
perturbation.explanation: The original text granted MD Anderson the right to use Inventions and Works for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes. The modified version adds the phrase "only with prior written approval from LBIO," which contradicts the previously granted right. Now, MD Anderson needs LBIO's approval, thus contradicting the free assignment of the rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Assignment of Inventions; Further Assurances. (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes only with prior written approval from LBIO]. MD Anderson further acknowledges and agrees that all original works of authorship that are made by MD Anderson (solely or jointly with others) in the performance of the Research, excluding any publication made in accordance with Section 6.4 (a "Work") and that are protectable by copyright are "works made for hire," as that term is defined in the United States Copyright Act. However, to the extent that any Work may not, by operation of any Laws, be a work made for hire, MD Anderson hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to such Work, including all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto, subject to MD Anderson's right to use such Work for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure only with prior written approval from LBIO.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted MD Anderson the right to use Inventions and Works for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes. The modified version introduces a requirement for prior written approval from LBIO for the same activities, which directly contradicts the previously granted right to use such inventions without needing LBIO's approval. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now reflects this contradiction as well. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction according to Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
